title: Avoid accessing Optional value without checking presence
description: Optional value can hold either a value or not. The value held in the Optional can be accessed using the get() method, but it will throw a NoSuchElementException if there is no value present. To avoid the exception, calling the isPresent() or ! isEmpty() method should always be done before any call to get().
recommendation: Always call isPresent() or ! isEmpty() before accessing the value of an Optional.

checks.message: Always call isPresent() or ! isEmpty() before accessing the value of an Optional.
